title: Formation Flight second-ranked common-offset boundary source discriminator
scientificQuestion: At the deterministic second-ranked common lattice offset, does the c16/c18/c20 population-weighted near-wing boundary source become smooth, remain mixed, or become non-asymptotic, and does averaging it with the accepted representative phase reduce h-linear curvature?

rationale.why: The accepted representative offset has smooth scalar geometry density but mixed direction-resolved population-weighted source curvature 0.8841533797. A second independently selected lattice phase is the minimum coupled experiment that can distinguish a phase-local residual from a repeatable lack of smoothness.
rationale.roi: The run reuses the unchanged five-cycle production TRT executable and exact source census. One c16/c18/c20 triplet costs about 82 minutes on the measured Apple M4 baseline and can stop an invalid multi-position formation-power ladder that would cost many times more.
rationale.solverEvolution: None. This experiment changes only the explicit diagnostic subcell translation from [0.25,0.25,0.75] to [0.5,0.75,0.5]. Collision, geometry, kinematics, force accounting, source reconstruction, cycles, phases, grids, and thresholds remain unchanged.

selectionRule.metric: sum over c16/c18/c20 of squared sample-SD-normalized distance from each resolution's leader areal-link-density median

geometryConditioning.status: recorded context, not an acceptance gate
geometryConditioning.reason: The authorized candidates were ranked by distance to the per-grid ensemble medians, not by endpoint-interpolation curvature. Direction-resolved areal, conditional-population, full-source, and component curvatures are therefore all retained so a nonsmooth full source is not silently attributed to population physics alone.
geometryConditioning.prohibitedInterpretation: A nonsmooth alternate full-source curvature by itself does not prove a defective boundary operator because lattice-phase geometry sampling may contribute.

decomposition.conditionalMomentumExchangePopulation: m_r(q) = (rawReflectedPopulationSum_r(q) + reconstructedIncomingPopulationSum_r(q)) / linkCount_r(q), with zero at zero support
decomposition.directionWeight: Euclidean length of each D3Q19 direction
decomposition.c18Expectation: linear interpolation of the c16 and c20 direction profiles at h18, where h = 1 / chordCells
decomposition.twoOffsetMean: For each grid and direction, arithmetic mean of the accepted representative-phase profile and this alternate-phase profile, followed by the same h-linear curvature calculation. It is a secondary robustness diagnostic, not a three-offset ensemble claim.

nextActions.smoothPopulationWeightedSource: run the final authorized deterministic offset [0.25,0,0.5], then preregister a three-offset mean and phase-spread acceptance rule before any power ladder
nextActions.mixedPopulationWeightedSource: run only the final authorized deterministic offset [0.25,0,0.5] to complete the minimal phase-robustness set; do not run a formation-power ladder
nextActions.populationWeightedSourceNonAsymptotic: stop the formation-power ladder and use the paired direction/component residuals to select one focused phase trace before deciding whether the final authorized offset adds information

claimBoundary: This second lattice phase can establish whether lack of smoothness repeats at two deterministic near-median offsets and can quantify a two-offset mean. It cannot establish three-offset robustness, quantitative formation-power convergence, a production correction, formation benefit, or a biological bird-flight claim.
